commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
By whose. Septuagint, "the powerful in Israel, that they might be sanctified to the Lord; and they put "Hebrew, "who instructed all Israel, the holy people of the Lord: Put "(Haydock)
No more. Hebrew adds, "upon your shoulders "(Haydock) whence it is inferred, that it had been carried about the cities of Juda, under the impious kings, who would not suffer it in the temple. (Calmet)
Some believe that it was kept in the house of Sellum, the uncle of Jeremias, and husband of Olda. (St. Jerome, Trad.) (Tirinus)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
